因為剛剛發生所以未有中文新聞報導。

Brexit vote TIED in House of Commons for first time in 26 years

A crunch vote on Brexit was tied in extraordinary scenes, the like of which have not been seen in the House of Commons in 26 years.

And the last vote to be tied in the Chamber was, ironically, in a vote on the Maastricht Treaty with the European Union.

The vote was on Labour MP Hilary Benn's bid to bring forward a third set of indicative votes to find a way forward on Brexit , which would have taken place on Monday.

After MPs voted on the plan, exactly 310 of them voted for and against it.

In the case of a tie, the Speaker has the casting vote.

Speaker John Bercow voted no, complying with the convention that when there is no majority in the House, the chair should not create one.

As such, the amendment was defeated by 311 votes to 310.

議長的解釋係「In accordance with precedent, and on the principle that important decisions should not be taken except by a majority, I cast my vote with the noes.」

冇Rules噃。有巴絲都講唔一定投反對或贊成。

最重要係個決定可以「延長議案討論」。

正如有巴絲講，係二讀時，議案打和，佢可以投贊成，令議案進入三讀，繼續進行討論。

而如果首讀打和，議長會而反對動議，等民間再就議案討論。因為首讀已經唔可以majority，即係成件議案行落去二讀或三讀都會有好大爭議。

如果持平議案係唔係都贊成通過，根據程序，已經進入行政階段。

當然你可以話入咗行政程序都可以提出議案否決。但個行政成本會有機高呢？英國對立法或改變本身法例會討論好耐。佢唔似美國咁，可以運用行政手段去控制法例嘅存在。佢地係幾尊重本身條條例同立法原意。
